title: The desert is not neutral: How heat and sand change weapons in war
content: As Iran's drones and ballistic missiles rain down on Gulf cities, oil terminals and airfields, a quieter battle is being fought against sand, heat and open sky — one that has reshaped the outcome of every conflict ever fought on Arabian soil.

title: 'Hey DM, where is CID?’: AIMIM neta's video threatening Bengal SIR officer viral
content: AIMIM leader Mofakrul Islam was arrested for his alleged role in the Malda violence, where judicial officers were held hostage. The Supreme Court criticized the West Bengal administration's failure to maintain law and order, ordering a central probe by the NIA. The incident has heightened political tensions ahead of the West Bengal elections.

title: Reason the 6am email from 'Oracle leadership' told thousands of laid-off employees
content: Oracle has begun its largest-ever round of layoffs, with employees receiving termination emails from "Oracle Leadership" at 6AM saying their roles had been eliminated after "careful consideration of Oracle's current business needs." Around 10,000 employees have reportedly been cut so far, with estimates suggesting the total could reach 30,000. The email gave employees a narrow window to act before systems were deactivated.

title: Chennai crowd forces Bishop to say 'sorry' during toss - Watch
content: The IPL 2026 toss between Chennai Super Kings and Punjab Kings was disrupted by a roaring Chepauk crowd, forcing Ian Bishop to apologise for the delays. Shreyas Iyer chose to bowl first. Ruturaj Gaikwad explained CSK’s opening strategy and pitch conditions as they aim to recover from a poor start.

title: Russia plans to step-up energy supply to India – Will it offset Middle East volatility?
content: Russia is ready to boost oil and liquefied natural gas supplies to India. This comes as the Middle East crisis creates global energy market volatility. Discussions between Russian and Indian officials covered expanding trade, investment, and industrial cooperation. Both nations also explored deepening engagement in civil nuclear energy. Russia confirmed its capacity for steady increases in energy shipments to India.

title: 'Can easily open Hormuz, take oil & make fortune': Trump makes 'gushing' claims
content: Trump's remark is concurrent to Washington's repeated claims that talks with Tehran have been "progressing well", though the Islamic regime, currently without the leadership, has outrightly quashed the Trump's claim. Trump's fresh assertion on Hormuz, a key chokepoint in the global oil supply route, comes a day after he appeared disgruntled that Western allies, particularly NATO, refused to shoulder their share in the war, which is nearing its fifth week after a joint US-Israeli aerial assault.

title: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff has a message for tech CEOs blaming AI for layoffs
content: Salesforce CEO Marc Benioff is pushing back on the narrative that AI is driving mass white-collar layoffs, calling it an oversimplification—and in some cases, a CEO's "lazy way out." While companies like Block and Atlassian have framed sweeping cuts as AI-driven restructuring, Benioff argues they're conflating three very different problems. At Salesforce, he's doing the opposite: hiring 20% more salespeople, convinced that human connection is something no AI can replicate.

title: 'Dhurandhar 2' BO day 16: Ranveer Singh film eyes Rs 950 crore
content: In just 16 days, 'Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge' has rewritten box office history, amassing a staggering Rs. 939.46 crore India net. The Aditya Dhar directorial, starring Ranveer Singh as the elite spy Hamza, has officially overtaken the lifetime collections of its predecessor in a mere 11 days. Despite the massive IPL 2026 season, the film continues to dominate theaters, crossing a global gross of Rs. 1,492.17 crore and becoming the highest-grossing Indian film of all time in North America. With powerful performances from Sanjay Dutt, Arjun Rampal, and Rakesh Bedi, this high-stakes espionage thriller is now rapidly closing in on the historic Rs. 1,000 crore India net milestone.

title: 'Keep your head on your shoulders': Yuvraj warns Sooryavanshi
content: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, 15, is emerging as a cricket prodigy after record-breaking IPL and Under-19 performances. Inspired by Brian Lara and Yuvraj Singh, he aims to deliver strong starts for Rajasthan Royals. Yuvraj praised his exceptional bat speed and all-round power, saying his India debut is inevitable, “not if, but when”, while stressing the importance of staying grounded.

title: Tamil Nadu elections: Why Annamalai missed BJP candidate list - alliance over leader?
content: K Annamalai's absence from the BJP's candidate list for the Tamil Nadu assembly elections has sparked speculation. Despite being sidelined, Annamalai pledged to support all NDA candidates aiming for a significant victory. His removal as state president and subsequent marginalization are linked to alliance dynamics with AIADMK and caste considerations.

title: Google now allows Gmail users to change username: What to know before changing it
content: Google now allows users to change their Gmail username, the part before @gmail.com, to update their digital identity. This feature, available in the US, lets users change their sign-in name for various Google services. The old username becomes an alternate email, and data remains unaffected, though some app settings might reset.

title: IPL 2026: Why is MS Dhoni not playing in CSK vs PBKS?
content: Chennai Super Kings are missing MS Dhoni due to a calf injury during their IPL 2026 clash with Punjab Kings, who chose to bowl first. After a poor opening loss, concerns remain, though coach Stephen Fleming said, “He is still heavily involved… his influence remains very strong.” CSK, led by Ruturaj Gaikwad, are rebuilding while also awaiting Dewald Brevis’ return from injury.

title: Five Indians among 12 injured by Iranian missile debris in Abu Dhabi
content: Debris from an intercepted Iranian missile injured at least 12 people in Abu Dhabi. Five Indian nationals were among those hurt. The incident occurred in the Ajban area. This follows rising tensions in the Gulf region. Churches in the UAE announced closures for Easter Sunday. Iran's Revolutionary Guard warned of attacks on US tech companies.

title: Bigger & quieter: What makes India's 3rd nuclear submarine INS Aridhaman deadly
content: India significantly bolstered its maritime defense with the commissioning of INS Aridaman, its third indigenous nuclear-powered ballistic missile submarine. This strategic induction enhances the nation's nuclear triad capabilities. Additionally, the frigate Taragiri, a testament to 'Make in India' and advanced shipbuilding, was also inducted, boosting naval combat readiness and operational might.

title: Praggnanandhaa’s invisible second: The making of Vaibhav Suri
content: FIDE Master Prasenjit Dutta, a former prodigy from Tripura, runs a chess academy in New Delhi. He mentored Vaibhav Suri, India’s 27th Grandmaster, whose dedication and positional mastery shaped his rise. Today, Suri focuses entirely on supporting young phenom R Praggnanandhaa, applying his deep strategic insight as a coach, analyst, and mentor behind the scenes of elite international chess.

title: 'They look committed': Yuvraj’s direct message to Gambhir-Agarkar on Rohit-Kohli future
content: "I don’t know what’s going on in their minds. They look committed to playing the World Cup, but I think that decision lies with them and the selectors, who will have to decide the future. My point is that they are such great players, there has to be clarity," Yuvraj said.

title: Why first tanker of Iran oil to India in 7 yrs was diverted to China
content: A US-sanctioned tanker carrying Iranian crude oil, initially bound for India, has rerouted to China. Payment issues appear to be the primary driver for this change, as sellers are reportedly tightening credit terms. This development highlights the ongoing challenges in facilitating Iranian oil trade, even with temporary US sanctions waivers.

title: 'Fraud Czar': Trump gives JD Vance new role; 'Blue States' in crosshairs
content: President Trump has appointed Vice President JD Vance as "Fraud Czar" to investigate fraud in federal benefit programs, particularly in Democrat-run states. This initiative aims to combat widespread theft of taxpayer money and could potentially balance the American budget. Raids have reportedly begun in Los Angeles as part of this effort.

title: France no longer hostile nation? After Macron snubs Trump, Iran allows ship to pass Hormuz
content: A French-owned container ship, the Kribi, crossed the Strait of Hormuz, potentially signaling a shift in Iran's view of France following President Macron's criticism of US policy. Macron rejected military intervention to reopen the strait, advocating for negotiations instead. The vessel's change of destination to "Owner France" may have signaled its nationality to Iranian authorities.

title: West Bengal polls: TMC reels in voters with 'Mache Bhate Bangali', leaves BJP high & dry
content: West Bengal's upcoming assembly polls see the common phrase 'Mache Bhate Bangali' (Bengali by fish and rice) become a potent political symbol. The TMC is leveraging this cultural identity against the BJP, which it accuses of imposing North Indian vegetarian culture. Fish, deeply ingrained in Bengali life, now represents pride and a defense against perceived cultural invasion.

title: Yuvraj’s candid revelation: ‘From nowhere Dhoni became captain’ despite him being VC
content: Yuvraj Singh revealed how captaincy can be unexpected, citing MS Dhoni's 2007 appointment despite senior players and his own vice-captaincy. He related this to modern debates, like Abhishek Sharma not leading his franchise, explaining player disappointment when consistent performers are overlooked. Dhoni's surprise captaincy, however, led to immense success for Indian cricket.

title: Tamil Nadu polls: BJP releases candidate list, drops K Annamalai amid seat row
content: The BJP has unveiled its candidate list for the 2026 Tamil Nadu Assembly elections, following internal discussions and cadre concerns. Notably, former state president K Annamalai is absent from this initial selection. Prominent figures like Tamilisai Soundararajan and Vanathi Srinivasan have been fielded from key constituencies, signaling the party's strategic approach for the upcoming polls.

title: Fact check: Does Ajinkya Rahane have 'one of the best strike rates since 2023'?
content: Kolkata Knight Riders captain Ajinkya Rahane defended his strike rate after a loss to Sunrisers Hyderabad, claiming he has one of the best since 2023. However, data reveals he ranks significantly lower overall, though his powerplay numbers are strong. His strike rate notably dips against spin and in middle overs.

title: 'Ghar wapsi soon': Imam chief's prediction on PoK, praises 'new Kashmir'
content: Chief Imam Umer Ahmed Ilyasi expressed confidence that Pakistan-Occupied Kashmir will soon rejoin India, citing the desire of its people and the positive developments in Jammu and Kashmir post-Article 370 abrogation. He highlighted a transformed Kashmir with increased prosperity and tourism, urging continued visits despite past incidents.

title: 'It's not okay': Yuvraj apologises to Kapil, Dhoni over father Yograj’s remarks
content: Yuvraj Singh publicly apologized to cricket legends Kapil Dev and MS Dhoni, distancing himself from his father Yograj Singh's controversial remarks. Yuvraj expressed deep respect for both captains, stating his father's comments do not reflect his own views. He emphasised his personal discomfort and desire to mend relationships, highlighting his own cricketing ties with Dhoni.

title: Andhra man enters home on pretext of water, rapes woman, inserts iron rod
content: A 35-year-old government employee in Macherla was severely injured after an unidentified man allegedly inserted an iron rod into her private parts. The accused reportedly raped and beat her before fleeing. The victim, found unconscious by her nephew and locals, is undergoing treatment and is out of danger. Police have formed special teams to apprehend the suspect.

title: 'Complete annihilation of Microsoft, Nvidia … ': Iran warns US after Trump's strike threats
content: Iran's Khatam al-Anbiya Headquarters has declared all regional ICT companies as legitimate targets, escalating conflict concerns. This follows reports of an Iranian strike damaging Amazon's cloud operations in Bahrain. Earlier, Iran's Revolutionary Guards had threatened US tech giants like Microsoft and Google, accusing them of aiding US strikes and acting as spies.

title: Kerala polls: Congress leader Ramesh Chennithala eyes CM post, awaits party's call
content: Congress leader Ramesh Chennithala stated that the party leadership will decide on the chief ministerial face for the 2026 Kerala Assembly elections, emphasizing his long-standing relationship with the Nehru family. He expressed confidence in the UDF's victory, highlighting their comprehensive manifesto and criticizing the LDF government's performance over the past decade.

title: Watch: Rahul enjoys 'appam, omelette' with UDF leaders ahead of Kerala polls
content: Congress MP Rahul Gandhi shared a video of him discussing Kerala's upcoming 2026 assembly elections with UDF leaders over local snacks. He emphasized the need for change, highlighting education reform, youth opportunities, and better social support. Gandhi expressed confidence in the UDF's readiness to lead, positioning them as renewed hope for the state.

title: 'Raghav Chadha is compromised': Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann on AAP's Rajya Sabha move
content: Punjab CM Bhagwant Mann defended Raghav Chadha's removal as AAP's Rajya Sabha deputy leader, citing party discipline and Chadha's alleged failure to raise critical issues.  Party leaders accused Chadha of not participating in opposition protests and prioritizing trivial matters over national concerns, suggesting he was compromised and hesitant to confront the central government.

title: ‘I was talking to the President’: Tiger Woods heard in DUI arrest video
content: Golf legend Tiger Woods, arrested on DUI charges after a car crash, was heard mentioning a conversation with the president.  Bodycam footage shows him disoriented, admitting he was distracted by his phone. Woods, who denied alcohol consumption, was found with prescription painkillers and refused a urine test, citing past injuries.

title: Broke? 5 money-saving homesteading trends that are slashing family expenses overnight
content: Families are embracing homesteading to save money and become more self-sufficient. Growing vegetables, raising chickens and making DIY products are popular. These skills offer immediate savings and protection against rising costs. Expert highlights how quality materials enhance these practices. Simple changes lead to significant financial benefits over time, building confidence and expanding into new areas.

title: With 170-seat target, Amit Shah to camp in Bengal for 15 days
content: Union Home Minister Amit Shah set a target of 170 seats for the Bengal BJP in the upcoming assembly elections, announcing he would camp in the state for 15 days. Shah expressed confidence that Suvendu Adhikari would win from Bhowanipore, Mamata Banerjee's home turf, calling it a 'shortcut' to regime change.

title: Why chef Sanjeev Kapoor was denied to wear chef coat for Padma Shri Award
content: Celebrity chef Sanjeev Kapoor was initially denied wearing his chef coat for the Padma Shri Award ceremony in 2017, being asked to wear traditional Indian attire instead. He insisted on wearing his uniform, which he considers a symbol of pride for the chef community. Ultimately, he received the award in his chef coat, setting a precedent.

title: Japan invites 1,000 Indian PhD scholars under LOTUS 2026
content: Japan's LOTUS Programme is now accepting applications for Indian Master's, PhD students, and postdoctoral researchers. Nearly 1,000 individuals can participate in funded research stays in Japan. The initiative aims to boost scientific collaboration between India and Japan. Applications are open until June 9, 2026, with research stays beginning in October 2026.

title: Energy crisis amid Iran tensions: Govt pushes to scale up induction heater production
content: Amidst rising energy costs and Middle East tensions, the Indian government is boosting domestic induction heater production to reduce reliance on gas-based cooking. An inter-ministerial meeting strategized scaling up manufacturing of induction appliances and cookware. This initiative, driven by the Prime Minister's directive for future planning, also involves expanding Piped Natural Gas coverage to further ease energy dependence.

title: ‘True player’: PM Modi hails Sanju Samson’s T20 World Cup heroics
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi lauded Sanju Samson's stellar performance in India's T20 World Cup 2026 victory, citing his match-winning impact and composure under pressure. Modi highlighted Samson's escalating focus and intensity during crucial knockout stages as exemplary. Samson's exceptional batting, including a record-breaking run tally, was instrumental in India's historic title defense on home soil.

title: 'He should resign first': Mamata targets PM Modi over voter list row in Bengal polls
content: West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee launched a sharp attack on PM Modi, demanding his resignation if infiltrators are on voter lists, as she questioned the electoral roll challenges. She also blamed AIMIM and ISF for the Malda judicial officers' gherao, accusing Congress and BJP of instigation.

title: Govt. targets to procure 23 lakh MT of Rabi paddy from April 4 in Andhra Pradesh
content: It will be procured through 1,917 paddy procurement centres, says Civil Supplies Minister Nadendla Manohar; farmers will be paid the amount within 24 hours of procurement, he adds

title: Ganderbal encounter: Jammu & Kashmir L-G Manoj Sinha orders magisterial inquiry
content: Jammu and Kashmir Chief Minister Omar Abdullah demanded “a transparent and time-bound probe with the facts made public”

title: U.P. ATS arrests four members of ‘terror module’ with links to Pakistan handlers
content: Besides Saqib, the arrested individuals have been identified as Vikas Gehlawat alias Raunak, Lokesh alias Papla Pandit alias Babu alias Sanju, both residents of Gautam Buddh Nagar, and Arbaab from Meerut.

title: YSRCP alleges denial of adequate speaking time in Rajya Sabha
content: A party delegation calls on Vice-President and Rajya Sabha Chairman C.P. Radhakrishnan, and complains to him over the issue

title: Grassroots surge lifts prospects of water sports in Visakhapatnam
content: District-level SAAP selections for sub-junior and junior categories feed into April 11-12 State meet at Punnami Ghat, as Rushikonda academy gathers pace and participation widens, says official

title: Participate in self-enumeration, appeals Union Minister Pralhad Joshi to public
content: They Minister says he has already uploaded the details of his family using the self-enumeration option

title: Trial run of one-way traffic around Hyderabad’s KBR Park on Sunday amid ongoing flyover work
content: Police say u-turns will not be permitted on the designated routes during the trial period and motorists are required to keep to the left to ensure smooth exits

title: Assembly elections LIVE: PM Modi holds roadshow in Puducherry
content: BJP announced 27 candidates, while Congress named candidates for 27 of the 28 constituencies for the T.N. Assembly election

title: Here are the big stories from Karnataka today
content: Welcome to the Karnataka Today newsletter, your guide from The Hindu on the major news stories to follow today. Curated and written by Nalme Nachiyar.

title: Union Minister launches BIRAC-BioNEST Incubation Centre at CFTRI
content: Hostel facility to come up for farmers, FPOs, and SHGs who participate in the training programmes offered by the CSIR-CFTRI

title: B.P. Yatnal, expelled BJP leader, begins campaign for BJP nominee in Bagalkot
content: He did not criticise Umesh Meti, the Congress candidate; instead, he trained his guns on Chief Minister Siddaramaiah

title: Karnataka governor asks State to review switch from marks to grades for third language in SSLC
content: A representation made to Governor Gehlot cautioned that a shift to a grading-only system may inadvertently diminish the academic importance of the subject and affect students’ motivation to engage seriously with it.

title: Habitual offender arrested in Nandyal, gold and cash seized
content: The accused was wanted in multiple offences reported from Nandyal, Bandi Atmakur, Revanur, Sanjamala, Gadivemula, and other parts of Rayalaseema, say police

title: All about Karnataka’s new Freedom Of Choice in Marriage Bill | Decode Karnataka
content: Karnataka Legislature recently passed the Karnataka Freedom of Choice in Marriage and Prevention and Prohibition of Crimes in the Name of Honour and Tradition (Eva Nammava, Eva Nammava) Bill, 2026.

title: Alleged mastermind behind Malda violence arrested, NIA team arrives
content: Moffakkerul Islam, a lawyer who had contested the 2021 polls on an AIMIM ticket, was seen giving a speech outside the BDO office and urging the crowd not to leave the premises; he was detained while trying to flee the State from Bagdogra airport in north Bengal

title: T.N. CM Stalin writes to PM Modi over FCRA Amendment Bill, terms it ‘draconian’
content: He pointed out that it sought to dismantle India’s charitable organisations by enabling the Union government, at its discretion, to cancel the FCRA registration of an organisation and take over its assets — whether it is a church, school, or hospital — “without any due process.”

title: Amaravati will be A.P. capital forever, law and courts will protect it, says CM
content: Majority of capital works will be over by end of 2028 and PM will be invited to inaugurate the iconic buildings and trunk infrastructure in 2029, asserts Chandrababu Naidu

title: Yettinahole: VJNL hopeful of supplying water to Tumakuru by October, and Kolar and Chikkaballapur next year
content: The rainfall data collected since 2017 shows average availability of 18 tmcft of water from the streams in the Western Ghats; VJNL claims the water available is more than sufficient for drinking purposes.

title: Kerala polls: CPI(M) sends legal notice to K.C. Venugopal over allegations of fake Aadhaar cards
content: The move comes in the wake of Venugopal’s public allegation that fake Aadhaar cards were being produced in CPI(M) Payyanur area committee office as part of a plan to manipulate the electoral process

title: Minister Payyavula Keshav invites Arvind Limited to set up garment units in Anantapur
content: The Finance Minister and the firm’s representatives discuss investment prospects and opportunities in Anantapur region

title: LPG crisis sparks surge in interest for electric autos in Bengaluru
content: The recent surge in fuel prices, triggered by West Asia conflict, has increased operating costs for conventional fuel-based vehicles. This has, in turn, revived attention towards electric vehicles (EVs), including electric cars, autos, two-wheelers and plug-in hybrids.

title: Telangana aspires to be textile capital of South Asia by 2047: CM
content: If you have a vision, we are your perfect partner for global success, says CM Revanth Reddy, wooing investors with assurances of best infrastructure, land, power, water, incentives and fastest execution of projects

title: NIMHANS review flags enduring impact of child suicides on parents, mental health professionals
content: The study finds that parents experience intense and prolonged grief marked by guilt, self-blame, and a persistent search for answers, while mental health professionals experience a parallel emotional burden

title: 55 history-sheeters bound over in Sri Sathya Sai district
content: In total, 325 persons were bound over in the district last year as part of the preventive policing measures, says SP

title: GNI project: Draft plan to ‘relocate’ project-affected Nicobarese families creates confusion
content: Absence of clarity on relocation sites, numbers, consent process raises concern among tribal communities.

title: Watch: Malda hostage incident rocks West Bengal politics, NIA starts investigation
content: West Bengal’s Malda district has witnessed a major law and order incident after seven judicial officers were held hostage by a mob for several hours. The episode has triggered police action, a Supreme Court intervention, and sharp political reactions. Here’s what happened in Malda.

title: In Focus podcast | West Bengal elections: How will SIR voter deletions and anti-incumbency shape outcomes?
content: West Bengal faces a high-stakes election as Mamata Banerjee battles anti-incumbency and voter deletions, with insights from Shiv Sahay Singh.

title: Government called ‘special session’ for political mileage in West Bengal, Tamil Nadu polls; it is MCC violation: Congress
content: Congress general secretary in-charge communications Jairam Ramesh said "we do not want any disturbance in the present differentials and relative strength"

title: Himachal BJP demands probe by sitting High Court judge into Chester Hills housing ‘scam’ in Solan
content: On March 31, Chief Secretary Sanjay Gupta held a presser to dismiss the allegations against the Chester Hills housing project as baseless, and said that the truth will prevail once the investigation is complete.

title: India’s new nuclear powered submarine INS Aridhaman commissioned
content: As per sources in the Navy, the commissioning was not made public and was carried out quietly, but Mr. Singh dropped enough hints to acknowledge the commissioning of the vessel

title: Russia offers to increase supply of crude oil, LNG to India amid war in West Asia
content: The energy cooperation figured prominently in meetings Russia’s First Deputy Prime Minister Denis Manturov held with National Security Advisor Ajit Doval and External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar

title: Latest NFL draft buzz: What we're hearing on Jermod McCoy's pro day, the QB3 debate
content: How did the Tennessee CB perform at his pro day? Who is the third-best QB in this class? Our draft experts weigh in with new intel three weeks out.

title: Tiger told deputy: 'Was just talking to president'
content: After crashing his SUV last week in Florida, Tiger Woods took out his phone and told a deputy, "I was just talking to the president," according to body camera footage showing the golfer's arrest on a DUI charge.

title: Luka (hamstring) set for MRI after exiting loss
content: Lakers star Luka Doncic exited Thursday's loss to the Thunder early because of a strained left hamstring.

title: Royals' Jensen oversleeps, scratched from lineup
content: Catcher Carter Jensen was removed from the Royals' starting lineup for Thursday's game against the Twins after he overslept.

title: Gattuso leaves Italy job after World Cup woe
content: Gennaro Gattuso has left his role as Italy manager after overseeing their failure to qualify for a third consecutive World Cup.

title: Phil to miss Masters due to family health matter
content: Phil Mickelson announced Thursday that he won't play in next week's Masters and will be out for an extended period as his family continues to "navigate a personal health matter."

title: Sources: KU's Bidunga to test draft, enter portal
content: Flory Bidunga, who projects as a second-round pick, would be the top player in the portal among the more than 800 players so far who intend to enter.

title: Judge denies UVA QB Morris' request for 7th year
content: A Virginia circuit court judge denied Virginia quarterback Chandler Morris' request for a preliminary injunction to play a seventh season on Thursday.

title: ⚽ The top men's U21 soccer players in the world
content: For the eighth straight year, ESPN brings you our list of the game's next young stars aged 21 or under. Who is good enough to make it?

title: Could Sarah Strong be UConn's all-time best -- and a four-time champ?
content: Sarah Strong had a freshman season for the record books -- and then she got better. Is another fairytale ending on the way?

title: NBA MVP straw poll: Wemby closes the gap in our final award update
content: Can Wemby's late push unseat SGA? Tim Bontemps polls 100 NBA insiders for the latest MVP snapshot. Plus, a look at the race for top rookie.

title: Scouts debate the top of the NBA draft class: Peterson or Dybantsa at No. 1?
content: Fit over talent? Versatility over production? Scouts and execs break down the conversations happening.

title: The incredibly rare thing you probably missed during the Cubs' 2016 curse-breaking World Series win
content: While the Cubs were making history in Cleveland 10 years ago, one unique accomplishment flew well under the radar.

title: 'We want to pinch ourselves': Dusty May's full-circle return to the Final Four
content: Three years after helming FAU's Cinderella run to the national semifinals, May returns with Michigan.

title: Your men's Final Four guide: Illinois-UConn, Arizona-Michigan (with predictions!)
content: We run through every team's keys to advancing to the championship game -- and their potential fatal flaws.

title: Safety 'savant' Caleb Downs to test NFL's thirst for his position
content: Since 2000, only eight safeties have been drafted in the top 10. Downs could challenge conventional wisdom.

title: NHL playoff watch: The Flyers' path to the postseason
content: Philly needs a win Friday -- and some help, too. Plus, full updated playoff projections after a 14-game night.

title: Expansion draft projections: The top player available on every team
content: Portland and Toronto are on the clock to start building out their rosters. These players could be picks Friday.

title: Weekend predictions: Man City or Liverpool in FA Cup? Can Atlético play spoiler vs. Barça?
content: From a big-club clash in England to a potentially decisive LaLiga matchup, another wild weekend of action awaits as Julien Laurens picks out the best games to watch in Europe.

title: Premier League without set-piece goals: Arsenal still first?
content: We crunched the numbers on how the standings would look like without goals from corners, free kicks and more.

title: UConn on brink of dual title appearance brings 2014 to mind
content: UConn men's and women's basketball are both in the Final Four. Could the school make its first dual national championship appearance since 2014? The world was quite different back then.

title: Men's turnament picks: How to bet Saturday's national semifinal games
content: Will the Illini get revenge for the loss to UConn back in November? Is the total too high in the late game?

title: Alvarez a top-10 bat? Ohtani wins Cy Young? Perdomo for real? Don't be surprised
content: Eric Karabell looks into the crystal ball to tell fantasy baseball managers what might come to pass in the not-too-distant future.

title: Midseason review: Top players and teams halfway through the year
content: From LSU's struggles to Landon Hairston's impressive year, checking in on college baseball's top storylines midway through the year.

title: Transfer rumors, news: Four Premier League clubs want Villa star Rogers
content: Manchester United, Chelsea, Arsenal, and Liverpool are interested in Aston Villa midfielder Morgan Rogers. Transfer Talk has the latest.

title: Russia Offers To Increase Crude Oil, LNG Supplies To India As Iran War Drags On
content: A Russian readout of the First Deputy Prime Minister's engagements in New Delhi said special attention was accorded to bilateral cooperation in the oil and gas sector.

title: Raghav Chadha Updates: MP Says Raised Public Issues, Day After Rajya Sabha Demotion
content: Raghav Chadha Updates: He was once considered a close confidant of AAP chief Arvind Kejriwal and was among the youngest MPs.

title: Is NATO Still Run By US As Trump Calls It 'Paper Tiger', Threatens To Exit?
content: Trump's latest statement questioning NATO's role is linked to the organisation's refusal to back the US during the Iran war.

title: PM Modi Holds Grand Roadshow In Puducherry Ahead Of Assembly Polls
content: Earlier, on his arrival, he was accorded a rousing welcome by the BJP workers amid chants of 'Bharat Mata Ki Jai' and 'Jai Shri Ram'.

title: 55-Year-Old Man Trampled To Death By Wild Elephant In Chhattisgarh
content: A 55-year-old man was trampled to death by a wild elephant in Chhattisgarh's Gaurela-Pendra-Marwahi district on Friday, forest officials said.

title: Human Remains Found On Thai Ship Iran Attacked In Strait Of Hormuz In March
content: The Thai-flagged Mayuree Naree was struck in March while travelling through the strait after departing Khalifa port in the United Arab Emirates.

title: Myanmar Junta Chief Min Aung Hlaing Elected President In Parliamentary Vote
content: The 69-year-old general has had a torrid time in power since he toppled the administration of Nobel Peace Prize winner and put her under arrest.

title: Bangladesh Shuts Shops, Banks Early To Tackle Deepening Fuel Crisis
content: Office hours across the country have been cut by one hour, running from 9 am to 4 pm instead of the usual 9 am to 5 pm schedule.

title: Opinion: Opinion | In Tamil Nadu, Vijay Attempts A Hostile Takeover Of AIADMK's Very Soul
content: It is increasingly clear that the message being driven home is that Vijay is the natural political successor to Anna and MGR.

title: Rajnath Singh Commissions Indian Navy's Stealth Frigate INS Taragiri
content: Visakhapatnam, April 3 (IANS) The Indian Navy commissioned its stealth frigate INS Taragiri on Friday at a grand ceremony, attended by Defence Minister Rajnath Singh, marking a defining moment in India’s maritime security and sovereignty.

title: 'Destroyer Of Foes': About Aridhaman, Latest To Join India's Submarine Fleet
content: Aridhaman is equipped with a nuclear reactor that provides it with a continuous supply of energy, eliminating the need to surface to refuel.

title: Ex-US Soldier Earned $1.5 Million A Month For UAE Kill Jobs: Report
content: According to Anssaf Ali Mayo, a Yemeni lawmaker, Abraham Golan was hired as a mercenary to kill him back in 2015. He said it was part of a larger effort by the UAE to eliminate people who opposed them politically.

title: Who Is Mofakkerul Islam? Man Arrested In Connection With Malda Hostage Case

title: Why Big Ten dominance might be here to stay
content: coach dusty May asked how recent rule changes in college athletics helped league teams have such success . this narrative ignores many past scandals in the league, let alone that the Big Ten has produced plenty of contenders through the years . the overall sentiment is somewhat fair, however, though .

title: Griffin joins Skenes, Ohtani on list of hyped MLB debuts
content: Konnor Griffin will make his major league debut against the Baltimore Orioles on friday . the 19-year-old is set to be the first player to make his MLB debut as a teenager since 2018 . he is the first teenager to suit up for the Pirates since 1998 .

title: Men's Final Four has a Carolina blue backdrop

title: 'Let her play': Chiefs launch petition for high school girls' flag football in Kansas
content: the Kansas City Chiefs announced an advertising campaign on friday . the campaign is aimed at persuading the KSHSAA to make girls' flag football a sanctioned sport in the state . in a 55-second video, the Chiefs showcased several girls who already play the sport .

title: Konnor Griffin is headed to the majors! What you need to know about Pirates' 19-year-old phenom
content: the Pittsburgh Pirates are calling up the top prospect in the sport . the 19-year-old shortstop is expected to become MLB's first teenage position player since Juan Soto debuted in 2018 . he is the best prospect baseball has seen in years .

title: Two-time All-Pro, 2019 DPOY Gilmore retiring
content: two-time All-Pro cornerback Stephon Gilmore announced his retirement on friday . the 35-year-old was selected a first-team All-pro in both 2018 and 2019 . he led the NFL with six interceptions and 20 passes defended .
